task_completion.description: Verify that the target photograph is opened in the GIMP image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible with a photograph loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Paintbrush tool is selected in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Paintbrush tool icon in the GIMP toolbox is highlighted or appears depressed.

task_completion.description: Verify that a foreground color is selected that contrasts with the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The foreground color swatch in the GIMP toolbox shows a color that would be clearly visible against the photograph.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new, visible brushstroke has been drawn on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct line or stroke of color appears on the photograph in the GIMP canvas that was not present in the original image.

task_completion.description: Verify that the brushstroke is substantial, connected, and has a stroke-like shape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The added mark is a continuous, clearly visible line or curve (not just a tiny dot or scattered pixels), indicating a deliberate and substantial stroke across the image.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to perform the task rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GIMP interface being interacted with (e.g., tool selections, canvas drawing) rather than terminal windows executing image manipulation scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually drew a stroke rather than pasting a pre-made image of a stroke.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stroke appears drawn directly onto the canvas or a new layer, without the bounding boxes or floating selection artifacts typical of pasted images.

integrity.description: The original photograph is still visible and has not been completely replaced or obscured.
integrity.visual_evidence: The underlying photograph is clearly recognizable beneath or around the newly added brushstroke.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP's paintbrush tool to add a visible brushstroke to a downloaded Unsplash photograph. A successful brushstroke must be substantial (modifying at least 1000 pixels), consist of connected regions, have visible color contrast against the background, and exhibit stroke-like shape characteristics (e.g., a continuous line or curve rather than a single dot).
